title: phragmites australis subsp. humilis
intro: Phragmites australis subsp. humilis, also known as dwarf or low-growing reed grass, is a type of perennial plant belonging to the Poaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to provide several health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and supporting respiratory health. Phragmites australis subsp. humilis can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional cooking and as a natural dye.
